securityPolicy.title: Security Policy
securityPolicy.description: Control how the AI agent handles tool execution. Configure permission rules with last-match-wins semantics.
securityPolicy.approvalTimeoutDesc: Seconds to wait for your approval before auto-resolving a tool call.
securityPolicy.timeoutBehaviorDesc: Action to take when approval times out. "Deny" rejects the tool call; "Allow" auto-approves it.
securityPolicy.rulesDesc: Define permission rules for tool execution. Uses fnmatch syntax. Later rules override earlier ones (last-match-wins).
securityPolicy.noRules: No permission rules configured. Default rules apply (terminal commands and external tools require approval).
securityPolicy.blacklistDesc: These dangerous command patterns are always blocked and cannot be overridden.
securityPolicy.pathPolicyDesc: Control which filesystem paths the AI agent can access. Forbidden paths are always blocked.
securityPolicy.forbiddenPathsDesc: These sensitive paths are always blocked and cannot be overridden by any configuration.
securityPolicy.allowedRootsDesc: Additional filesystem paths the agent is allowed to access (outside workspace).
securityPolicy.noAllowedRoots: No additional paths configured. Agent can only access workspace directory.

privacy.title: PII Privacy Protection
privacy.description: Detect and handle personally identifiable information (PII) in conversations. Supports automatic redaction and blocking of sensitive data.
privacy.enableDesc: Automatically detect phone numbers, ID cards, bank cards, emails and other PII in messages and tool calls.

routing.title: Privacy-Aware Model Routing
routing.description: Route LLM requests to a local model when sensitive data is detected, keeping private data off the cloud.
routing.noLocalModelWarning: You have selected a local routing strategy but no local model is configured. Routing will fall back to cloud for all requests.

advanced.title: Advanced PII Configuration
advanced.description: Define custom keywords, regex patterns, and sensitive tools to extend the built-in PII detection rules.
advanced.keywordsS2Desc: Words that should be treated as sensitive (S2). Minimum 2 characters.
advanced.keywordsS3Desc: Words that should be treated as confidential (S3).
advanced.patternsS2Desc: Regex patterns to detect sensitive content.
advanced.patternsS3Desc: Regex patterns to detect confidential content.
advanced.toolsS2Desc: Tool calls that should trigger S2 protection.
advanced.toolsS3Desc: Tool calls that should trigger S3 protection.
advanced.keywordTooShort: Keyword must be at least 2 characters
advanced.testMatchPlaceholder: Enter text to test against your rules...

privacy.pseudonymizeHint: Pseudonymize mode replaces PII with typed semantic placeholders before sending to the cloud. The AI retains full semantic understanding while original values are restored locally. Zero privacy leakage, zero quality loss.
privacy.deepScanDesc: Use LLM to detect non-structured PII (medical history, family relations, workplace, etc.) that regex patterns cannot catch. May increase latency.

domainAllowlist.title: Domain Allowlist
domainAllowlist.description: Control which domains the AI agent can access via browser and network tools. Only listed domains are allowed.
domainAllowlist.hitlDesc: When enabled, accessing unlisted domains will prompt for your approval instead of being blocked directly.

domainAllowlist.invalidDomain: Invalid domain format. Use patterns like example.com or *.example.com

autoReview.title: Smart Intent Guard
autoReview.description: Use an LLM to automatically review high-risk tool calls (like shell commands or network requests) against your original intent. If the action matches your intent, it is silently approved, reducing interruption fatigue.

autoReview.shellEscalationHint: Note: In Smart Intent Guard mode, high-risk operations (e.g. shell commands) will be reviewed by the security model even if your permission rules set them to "Allow". Trivially safe commands (ls, cat, git status, etc.) are fast-tracked without LLM review.

relational_store.label: Relational store
relational_store.evidence: Checks whether SQLite/relational memory storage is available so Profile, Procedural, and approval queues can read and write reliably.

memory_base_path.label: Memory path permissions
memory_base_path.evidence: Checks whether the local memory path or its parent is writable across Local Web, Tauri desktop, and SaaS sandbox volume mounts.

vector_index.label: Vector index
vector_index.evidence: Checks whether the semantic recall backend is available so cross-session memories can be retrieved by meaning.

knowledge_graph.label: Knowledge graph
knowledge_graph.evidence: Checks whether relationship reasoning is available; recall still works without it, but relational explanations are weaker.

embedding_provider.label: Embedding provider
embedding_provider.evidence: Checks whether embedding mode is available so new writes can build vector indexes.

event_ledger.label: Event ledger
event_ledger.evidence: Checks whether Command Center observability can persist trace, audit, and replay evidence.

health_snapshot.label: Health snapshot
health_snapshot.evidence: Checks whether memory health snapshot caching is available to avoid heavy scans on every console open.

deployment_boundary.label: Deployment boundary
deployment_boundary.evidence: Checks that Local Web, Tauri desktop, and SaaS sandbox share the same content isolation boundary.

context_bundle_manifest.label: Context bundle manifest
context_bundle_manifest.evidence: Checks whether the unified context bundle manifest exists and the volume layout is writable for memory, workspace, and offload scenes.

embedding_live.label: Embedding live probe
embedding_live.evidence: Runs a real embedding request so semantic indexing is proven beyond static configuration.

retrieval_pipeline.label: Retrieval pipeline
retrieval_pipeline.evidence: Runs a content-safe retrieval check to verify search, fusion, and trace capture.

sparse_cjk_recall.label: Sparse CJK recall
sparse_cjk_recall.evidence: Runs a CJK keyword query to verify the BM25/RRF sparse recall path.

golden_recall_benchmark.label: Golden recall benchmark
golden_recall_benchmark.evidence: Temporarily writes synthetic semantic and episodic memories, retrieves them, then cleans probe data to verify top-k recall for new memories.

memory_quality_governance.label: Memory quality governance
memory_quality_governance.evidence: Computes freshness, coverage, retention health, and coherence quality signals so long-term memory is governed rather than only accumulated.

migration_integrity.label: Migration integrity
migration_integrity.evidence: Checks applied migration checksums against the current migration manifest so schema drift is visible before startup or import rollback fails.

run_diagnostics.dryRun: Runs read-only probes and records content-free audit events.
run_diagnostics.expectedEffect: Refreshes probe evidence and confirms whether dependencies recovered.

run_health_refresh.dryRun: Recomputes local memory quality metrics and updates the health cache.
run_health_refresh.expectedEffect: Updates freshness, coverage, retention health, and coherence signals in the GUI.

review_storage_config.dryRun: Checks point to a local storage, database, or permission issue.
review_storage_config.expectedEffect: Restores memory writes, replay events, and diagnostic persistence after operator action.

enable_vector_store.dryRun: Vector-backed semantic and episodic recall is unavailable or degraded.
enable_vector_store.expectedEffect: Restores high-recall semantic search after vector storage and embeddings are configured.

configure_embedding.dryRun: Embedding checks failed or no embedding provider is configured.
configure_embedding.expectedEffect: New memories can be embedded, indexed, and recalled by semantic similarity.

review_retrieval_trace.dryRun: Reads existing retrieval trace metadata without exposing recalled memory content.
review_retrieval_trace.expectedEffect: Identifies which retrieval stage stopped producing trace or recall evidence.

error.0: Another silly mistake? Need me to hold your hand?
error.1: Don't look at me, I'm just a cute pet. I can't fix your bad logic.
error.2: Let me guess... Another null pointer? Truly a masterpiece.
error.3: An error? Well, it's not like you had sleep plans anyway.

success.0: Took you long enough. Fine, good job I guess.
success.1: It actually worked? I guess miracles do happen.
success.2: All done? Great, now go get me a coffee.
success.3: Passed, but I bet there are at least five TODOs left in there.

general.0: Are you still typing? I'm literally falling asleep.
general.1: I hope your logic is as sharp as my claws.
general.2: Honestly, you spend more time writing comments than actual code.
general.3: Just watching you write this code with deep skepticism.

error.0: Interesting anomaly. Let's trace the stack!
error.1: Linter is screaming. Check the line numbers immediately.
error.2: Exception detected! We might need a rollback here.
error.3: This bug is clever. A full AST traversal might be needed.

success.0: Zero errors. All green. Perfect logical flow.
success.1: All tests passed! This is the ultimate triumph of refactoring.
success.2: Excellent, the logic has converged with zero performance jitter.
success.3: Verification completed. All edge cases perfectly covered.

general.0: Scanning codebase... System state is currently nominal.
general.1: I find the cyclomatic complexity of this module highly elegant.
general.3: Stay calm, we still have several files left to optimize.

error.0: Don't worry, every error is just a step closer to success.
error.1: Breathe in, breathe out. No rush, we will fix this together.
error.2: It's just a temporary hurdle. Take a sip of water and reset.
error.3: It's just a little shy, let's gently add a null check for it.

success.0: Wonderful! Your patience has been beautifully rewarded.
success.1: Seeing it pass feels so peaceful and healing.
success.2: You did so well. Every step was incredibly solid.
success.3: All that patience paid off. I'm so happy for you!

general.0: Don't worry, I'm right here with you.
general.1: Tired? Close your eyes and rest for a minute.
general.2: Take your time. High-quality code takes time to grow.
general.3: Listen to the rhythm of your keys. It's quite peaceful.

error.0: Errors are the portals of discovery.
error.1: A wise coder reflects before they edit. See this as a chance.
error.2: Don't just look at the red line; see the architectural shift it hints.
error.3: Every bug's birth points towards a more elegant path.

success.0: Harmony restored. The architecture aligns once more.
success.1: Perfection is not when there is more to add, but nothing left to remove.
success.2: You found the root cause. A beautiful moment of clarity.
success.3: Thought and action as one. The code flows with your wisdom.

general.0: The best code is no code at all.
general.1: Refactoring is not changing behavior; it is an act of reflection.
general.2: Every line you write today shapes the system of tomorrow.
general.3: Be still. The code is just a reflection of the mind.

error.0: Let's just delete the whole file and start over!
error.1: Oops! Did something blow up? Fun! Let's check the console!
error.2: Quick! Change all ports and API keys, see if magic happens!
error.3: Alert! Absolute chaos! Mash the keyboard to save the day!

success.0: Yay! It passed! Now let's write a recursive loop to celebrate!
success.1: It worked? Awesome! Push directly to master right now!
success.2: Too fast! Let's write an infinite loop that melts the CPU next!
success.3: Success! Let's throw in some magic numbers that no one understands!

general.0: What happens if we pull this cable? Let's find out!
general.1: Close your eyes and mash the keys, it might compile better!
general.2: Let's skip all unit tests today and trust in fate!
general.3: Look! A butterfly flapped its wings, stack overflow imminent!
